original_abstract: This article examines the defining characteristics of contemporary globalization through the framework of global value chains (GVCs), deriving both positive and normative implications for the economies of Central and Eastern Europe. Specifically, we delineate the nature of the structural challenges these countries currently face and evaluate the strategic directions necessary for future public policy. Particular emphasis is placed on the role of technological advancement as a primary driver for securing high-value-added activities. These theoretical explorations are reinforced by empirical evidence, utilizing GVC integration indices and a comprehensive synthesis of current economic literature to support our findings.
humanized_abstract: Globalization of today can be understood with the help of global value chains (GVCs) and we derive pros and cons for the Central and Eastern European (CEE) economies. The goal of this article is to outline the challenges that countries in the region are facing and to signal the strategic choices for public policy in order to address them properly. We concentrate on the technological factor which is responsible for the decisions concerning high value adding activities in the GVCs. We refer to appropriate indicators and GVC integration indicators along with literature review in order to confirm our theoretical conclusions.
